tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, and ketonuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection. Low HbA1c with glucosuria and ketonuria may indicate recent acute metabolic disturbance (e.g., starvation, acute illness, or renal glycosuria) rather than chronic hyperglycemia. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for acute or chronic kidney disease and underlying metabolic derangements.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
